lesson.menu_desc: Legal Nouns & Defining Relative Clauses with that / which -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Contract Vocabulary -- Legal Nouns & Defining Clauses
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Simone Quiles de Santana Marques | Aula 11 -- Contract Vocabulary

listenings.text: Hi Simone, this is Daniel from the legal team. I am calling about the new contract with our partner in Germany. Before our review meeting, please read the document carefully. There is one clause that worries me. It is the clause that sets the liability if either party fails to deliver on time. The other party wants a very high limit, and I think that is a risk for us. Also, check the section that lists the obligations of each party, and the part that defines the warranty on the software. If you find any term that is not clear, mark it. A breach of this contract could be very expensive, so we want every clause to be precise. Let us talk after you read it. Thank you, and talk soon.

Hello, this is Simone. I am recording my notes on the new contract, so I do not forget anything before the review. First, there is a clause that limits the liability of each party, and I think the limit is too high for us. Second, the section that lists the obligations is clear, but the deadline that it sets is very tight. Third, the warranty that covers the software lasts only six months, and I would like a longer period. Finally, there is a term that defines a breach, and I want to make it more precise, because a breach that is not well defined creates risk. I will raise these points with the partner at our meeting on Thursday.

extra_audio.text: Daniel sends the new contract with the German partner and asks Simone to read it. Simone reads the document and marks every clause that is not clear. She notes the clause that limits the liability and the section that lists the obligations. She prepares her questions about the warranty and the definition of a breach. At the review meeting, Simone raises each point with the other party.
